title: Structural Exhaustion JSON Schema Bundle
description: Typed schemas for mathematical-obligation nodes, ordered tactic graphs, contract instances, complete tactic verifications, and branch execution traces. Every schema types and operationalizes a component of the structural exhaustion framework (branch_closure_methodology_extended.tex): the CT1-CT17 closure-tactic library, branch states B = (H_0, progress record, exclusion register E, invariant register I, residual condition R, vocabulary record V, payload queue Q, audit record A), the S-* lemma-schema alphabet with the package's A-* audit extensions, the certificate alphabet C1-C5, typed payload signatures P_{i->j}, ledgers, budgets, and the strategy-manual runtime model. JSON Schema validates local structure; tools/validate_tactics.py validates graph semantics.

informal.description: The required nonempty prose statement of the claim. It must be mathematically unambiguous and include the domains, hypotheses, quantifiers, and conclusion; a label or phrase such as 'remaining case' is insufficient.

latex.description: An optional LaTeX rendering of the same claim, using the notation of the structural exhaustion framework and not weakening or abbreviating the informal statement.

formalLanguage.description: When a machine-formal statement is supplied, the nonempty name and version of its formal language or proof assistant.

formal.description: When available, the machine-formal proposition or definition corresponding exactly to the informal and LaTeX statements.

mathStatement.description: A mathematical assertion carried by the proof graph. It must state the complete claim used at a proof-flow node, with all hypotheses, quantifiers, constants, and conclusions of the named definition, lemma, proposition, theorem, or branch guard. This operationalizes the framework's well-formedness discipline for branch states: a phrase such as 'the remaining case' is a branch state only after it has been converted into a typed statement over the current proof language.

name.description: The nonempty stable symbol or record key by which this mathematical object is referenced in the branch state's vocabulary record V and the evidence graph.

sort.description: The nonempty mathematical type of the referenced object, drawn from the sort system of the framework's proof language L and sufficiently precise to check consumers; typical sorts are ambient objects, pieces, boundary interfaces, outside contexts, local tests, witness spaces, external types, finite labels, ledgers, budgets, ranks, residual classes, payload tuples, certificates, and audit rows.

role.description: How the enclosing node uses the object: consumed input, produced output, preserved state, fixed parameter, certificate, or routed payload component.

definitionRef.description: When present, a source label or stable artifact reference to the definition that constructs the object from recorded data.

proofRef.description: When present, a source label or stable artifact reference proving the object's well-formedness or the fact represented by it.

typedRef.description: A reference to a mathematical object in the recorded branch state, together with its sort and data-flow role. It types one entry of the framework's vocabulary record V; typical entries are pieces (X, dX, iota_X), outside context classes, local test families, witness spaces, labels, external types, charge maps, budgets, rank quotients, offset sets, exact response profiles, and black-box theorem handles.

obligationId.description: A stable identifier, unique within the package, for this mathematical obligation; it may contain letters, digits, periods, underscores, and hyphens.

modality.description: The kind of discharge required: supply a definition, proof, computation, decision, construction, validation, or audit as selected by this value.

statement.description: The complete mathematical statement to be discharged at the node, not merely its display title.

contractSchemas.description: The nonempty set of S-* and A-* contracts that every successful proof record for this obligation must instantiate; no listed contract may be omitted or duplicated. The S-* names are the framework's lemma-schema alphabet (S-Def, S-Equiv, S-Dich, S-Pers, S-Det, S-Rout, S-Trig, S-Comp, S-Rest, S-Meas); S-Prove and the A-* names are package extensions operationalizing the framework's displayed propositions, certificate alphabet C1-C5, scope audit, and admission gates.

dependencies.description: Stable node, definition, theorem, computation, or audit identifiers whose certified conclusions are used by this obligation; the array is empty exactly when the obligation is logically independent of earlier package artifacts.

preconditions.description: Optional mathematical conditions that must already hold in the incoming branch state before this obligation may execute.

conclusion.description: Optional explicit postcondition produced on successful discharge; it must be the exact statement made available to downstream nodes.

dischargeCriteria.description: A nonempty operational criterion stating what proof object, computation, construction, or audit finding counts as completion, including satisfaction of every declared contract.

obligation.description: The exact mathematical work assigned to one proof-flow node. It identifies what must be defined, proved, computed, decided, constructed, validated, or audited before any outgoing branch may be followed.

transitionNumber.description: The transition's one-based presentation-order number within its tactic. It agrees with a legacy Enn suffix when present; semantic edge IDs are independent of this ordinal. Transition numbers must be consecutive under semantic validation.

transitionId.description: The stable edge identifier, either a legacy source ordinal <tacticId>.E<zero-padded number> or a semantic runtime ID <tacticId>.edge.<name>.

fromNodeId.description: The identifier of the declared source node; it must resolve to a node in the same tactic.

toNodeId.description: The identifier of the declared target node; it must resolve to a node in the same tactic.

transitionType.description: The semantic role of the edge, following the framework's tactic-diagram line grammar: intended proof flow (solid), inter-tactic payload handoff requiring S-Rout at the source and S-Trig at the target (dotted), repairable design-failure recovery (dashed), candidate-scope-obstruction referral to the global audit (red-dashed), verification check, or declared loop step certified by S-Rest and S-Meas.

guard.description: A nonempty branch-state-measurable condition that must hold before this edge may be traversed. For a decision node, sibling guards must describe the proved exhaustive alternatives.

guardLatex.description: An optional LaTeX rendering of the guard, or null when the source has no mathematical display for it.

sourceStyle.description: Optional source-diagram style or line grammar used for traceability; it must identify the visual edge style without replacing transitionType.

branchTag.description: Optional stable constructor-like tag naming the decision result that activates this transition; semantic runtime graphs use it to match outgoing edges to decision alternatives.

resultConstructor.description: Optional fully qualified result-constructor name whose proof-carrying value activates this edge.

transitionConstructor.description: Optional fully qualified evidence-carrying transition constructor represented by this edge.

evidenceSlots.description: Ordered typed evidence carried by the transition constructor; dependent indices should be listed before values whose types mention them.

transition.description: A directed proof-flow edge in a CT tactic diagram of the structural exhaustion framework. It records the source and target obligations, the branch-state-measurable condition under which the edge is legal, and its role in the framework's line grammar: ordinary execution, payload handoff, design-failure recovery, scope referral, check, or well-founded loop.

artifactType.description: The node artifact discriminator; it must be exactly 'nodeSpec'.

schemaVersion.description: The version of the node-specification format used by this artifact; it must match the containing tactic/library version.

nodeNumber.description: The node's one-based presentation-order position in its tactic, independent of the order in which a particular branch executes nodes. It agrees with a legacy Nnn suffix when present; semantic runtime IDs are independent of this ordinal.

nodeId.description: The stable node identifier, either a legacy source ordinal <tacticId>.N<zero-padded number> or a semantic runtime ID ending in entry, decide.<name>, certify.<name>, or terminal.<case>.

localKey.description: A nonempty tactic-local mnemonic matching the source diagram key or equivalent stable local name.

nodeType.description: The node's mathematical/data-flow role in the framework's tactic diagrams. It determines which specialized data are required: decisions need branches proved exhaustive by S-Dich, payload constructors need typed payload data with S-Rout, certificates need C1-C5 certificate data, handoffs need consumer data checked by S-Trig, scope audits and admission gates need findings, and loop measures need S-Meas measure data.

runtimeKind.description: Optional control-machine classification used by semantic runtime graphs. It separates entry, branching decision, single-successor certification, and terminal states from the broader mathematical nodeType taxonomy.

terminalCase.description: Optional exact terminal-case discriminator. Semantic graphs retain distinct values for mathematically different exits even when they share a certificate class or consumer tactic.

title.description: A nonempty human-readable title naming the exact definition, lemma, comparison, state, decision, or terminal finding represented by the node.

labelLatex.description: Optional LaTeX copied from or faithfully representing the source diagram label.

obligation.description: The exact typed mathematical obligation that must be discharged before any outgoing transition is legal.

inputs.description: The ordered typed objects read from the incoming branch state; include every object used by the node and leave the array empty only when it consumes none.

outputs.description: The ordered typed objects certified or constructed by the node for downstream use; include every new object and leave the array empty only when it produces none.

dependsOn.description: The unique node IDs whose discharged conclusions are prerequisites of this node; all IDs must resolve within the proof package or an explicitly imported dependency graph.

outgoingTransitionIds.description: The unique IDs of every transition leaving this node. The set must equal the graph's actual outgoing edges, including every decision branch and recovery route.

terminalRole.description: The node's sink classification under the framework's leaf-totality rule: none for a nonterminal, otherwise a C1-C5 certificate sink, a consumer handoff carrying a typed payload P_{i->j}, a candidate-scope-obstruction audit, or an admission rejection. A terminal branch is legal exactly when it returns one of these typed outcomes; a prose residual is not a terminal leaf.

transitionId.description: The ID of the outgoing transition implementing this alternative; it must be listed in outgoingTransitionIds.

guard.description: The branch-state-measurable mathematical condition defining this alternative.

targetNodeId.description: The node reached when this alternative holds; it must equal the target of transitionId.

resultConstructor.description: Optional fully qualified proof-carrying result constructor selecting this branch.

evidenceSlots.description: Ordered typed evidence retained by this branch and consumed by its transition or target state.

branches.description: For a decision node, the complete array of branch alternatives whose exhaustiveness over the residual class C(B) is an S-Dich obligation, each tied to one outgoing transition and target. It is required whenever nodeType is 'decision'.

payloadId.description: The stable identifier of the typed residual payload constructed on this route, matching a payload signature P_{i->j} in the payload component of the framework's proof language.

consumerTacticId.description: The unique tactic CTj authorized to consume this payload after its S-Trig checks pass; uniqueness of the declared consumer in the interlock graph is part of the producer's S-Rout obligation.

routeClass.description: Whether this payload is an intended residual handoff or a repair/recovery handoff.

alternatives.description: The nonempty set of payload routes the constructor may produce; each route must name a stable payload, one consumer, and whether it is intended or recovery flow.

tupleComponents.description: The ordered typed tuple carried by the payload; its arity and sorts must match both the producer's S-Rout instance and the consumer's trigger signature.

payload.description: For a payload-constructor node, the complete typed routing specification realizing the framework's legal-payload-exit rule: a non-certificate branch exit is complete precisely when it produces a legal payload instance with a declared consumer. It is required whenever nodeType is 'payload_constructor'.

class.description: The terminal class from the framework's certificate alphabet: C1 direct target certificate (realized local test plus the target-test equivalence), C2 minimality or compression certificate (admissible smaller object or replacement under the well-founded order), C3 routed defect certificate (defect account with an accepting consumer), C4 bounded-capacity certificate (impossible ledger, budget, rank, tier, rate, or aggregate inequality), or C5 finite verified impossibility certificate (audited exhaustion of a finite table, alphabet, or survivor list).

validationStatement.description: The exact theorem, inequality, finite exhaustion, or routed-defect condition that must be proved before this certificate may close the branch.

certificate.description: For a certificate node, the terminal certificate class and exact validation requirement; the certificate alphabet C1-C5 is the terminal return type of the framework's tactic library. It is required whenever nodeType is 'certificate'.

tacticId.description: The stable identifier of the receiving tactic CTj whose trigger signature must accept the payload; S-Trig checks arity, component types, standing hypotheses, interface data, and any required certificate handles before the consumer begins.

triggerContract.description: The mandatory consumer-side contract discriminator; it must be exactly 'S-Trig'.

consumer.description: For a consumer-handoff node, the receiving tactic and mandatory trigger contract. It is required whenever nodeType is 'consumer_handoff'.

classification.description: The classification discriminator; it must be exactly 'candidate_scope_obstruction'. Under the framework's repair protocol a candidate becomes a scope obstruction only after the language-extension audit rules out a level-4 proof-language extension, so this value does not itself establish a scope obstruction.

requiredFinding.description: The precise non-expressibility result the framework's global scope audit must establish: that no finite descriptor or level-4 proof-language extension can encode the residual in the current proof language.

scopeAudit.description: For a scope-audit node, the candidate obstruction classification and the non-expressibility finding still required. It is required whenever nodeType is 'scope_audit'.

classification.description: The classification discriminator; it must be exactly 'execution_reject'.

requiredFinding.description: The exact failed admission condition that must be recorded before execution is rejected, drawn from the framework's admission gates: a missing both-sides field (payer/account, bounded residual, measurable branch datum, typed consumer route) or a bookkeeping-vs-theorem failure.

auditGate.description: For an admission/audit-gate node, the execution-rejection classification and failed-condition finding. It is required whenever nodeType is 'audit_gate'.

name.description: The stable name of the progress measure, which must be definable from recorded branch-state data.

wellFoundedOrder.description: The domain, codomain, and strict well-founded relation used to order measure values.

strictDecreaseRequired.description: The termination invariant; it must be true, requiring strict decrease on every non-closing pass.

measure.description: For a loop-measure node, the named measure, its well-founded order, and the strict-descent requirement; this is the graph-level form of the framework's S-Meas obligation, which together with S-Rest certifies termination of iterative tactics such as CT12 (Peeling loop). It is required whenever nodeType is 'loop_measure'.

file.description: The repository-relative source-document path containing the corresponding definition, proof-flow node, or diagram node.

section.description: The source section, theorem, figure, or proof-flow step that prescribes the node's mathematical content.

line.description: A one-based source line locating the node or its defining text; it is traceability metadata, not a mathematical dependency.

diagramKey.description: Optional stable TikZ/source key for the corresponding diagram node.

source.description: The location in the structural exhaustion framework document from which the node specification was extracted or against which it was reviewed.

tikzStyles.description: TikZ style tokens attached to the source node; these affect presentation only and must not encode mathematical semantics absent from nodeType.

coordinates.description: The source diagram coordinates as text, retained for layout reproduction only.

presentation.description: Optional non-normative rendering metadata retained to reproduce or cross-check the source diagram.

nodeType.description: The node's mathematical/data-flow role in the framework's tactic diagrams. It determines which specialized data are required: decisions need branches proved exhaustive by S-Dich, payload constructors need typed payload data with S-Rout, certificates need C1-C5 certificate data, handoffs need consumer data checked by S-Trig, scope audits and admission gates need findings, and loop measures need S-Meas measure data.

The node's mathematical/data-flow role in the framework's tactic diagrams. It determines which specialized data are required: decisions need branches proved exhaustive by S-Dich, payload constructors need typed payload data with S-Rout, certificates need C1-C5 certificate data, handoffs need consumer data checked by S-Trig, scope audits and admission gates need findings, and loop measures need S-Meas measure data.

terminalRole.description: The node's sink classification under the framework's leaf-totality rule: none for a nonterminal, otherwise a C1-C5 certificate sink, a consumer handoff carrying a typed payload P_{i->j}, a candidate-scope-obstruction audit, or an admission rejection. A terminal branch is legal exactly when it returns one of these typed outcomes; a prose residual is not a terminal leaf.

nodeType.description: The node's mathematical/data-flow role in the framework's tactic diagrams. It determines which specialized data are required: decisions need branches proved exhaustive by S-Dich, payload constructors need typed payload data with S-Rout, certificates need C1-C5 certificate data, handoffs need consumer data checked by S-Trig, scope audits and admission gates need findings, and loop measures need S-Meas measure data.

terminalRole.description: The node's sink classification under the framework's leaf-totality rule: none for a nonterminal, otherwise a C1-C5 certificate sink, a consumer handoff carrying a typed payload P_{i->j}, a candidate-scope-obstruction audit, or an admission rejection. A terminal branch is legal exactly when it returns one of these typed outcomes; a prose residual is not a terminal leaf.

nodeType.description: The node's mathematical/data-flow role in the framework's tactic diagrams. It determines which specialized data are required: decisions need branches proved exhaustive by S-Dich, payload constructors need typed payload data with S-Rout, certificates need C1-C5 certificate data, handoffs need consumer data checked by S-Trig, scope audits and admission gates need findings, and loop measures need S-Meas measure data.

terminalRole.description: The node's sink classification under the framework's leaf-totality rule: none for a nonterminal, otherwise a C1-C5 certificate sink, a consumer handoff carrying a typed payload P_{i->j}, a candidate-scope-obstruction audit, or an admission rejection. A terminal branch is legal exactly when it returns one of these typed outcomes; a prose residual is not a terminal leaf.

nodeType.description: The node's mathematical/data-flow role in the framework's tactic diagrams. It determines which specialized data are required: decisions need branches proved exhaustive by S-Dich, payload constructors need typed payload data with S-Rout, certificates need C1-C5 certificate data, handoffs need consumer data checked by S-Trig, scope audits and admission gates need findings, and loop measures need S-Meas measure data.

terminalRole.description: The node's sink classification under the framework's leaf-totality rule: none for a nonterminal, otherwise a C1-C5 certificate sink, a consumer handoff carrying a typed payload P_{i->j}, a candidate-scope-obstruction audit, or an admission rejection. A terminal branch is legal exactly when it returns one of these typed outcomes; a prose residual is not a terminal leaf.

nodeType.description: The node's mathematical/data-flow role in the framework's tactic diagrams. It determines which specialized data are required: decisions need branches proved exhaustive by S-Dich, payload constructors need typed payload data with S-Rout, certificates need C1-C5 certificate data, handoffs need consumer data checked by S-Trig, scope audits and admission gates need findings, and loop measures need S-Meas measure data.

The node's mathematical/data-flow role in the framework's tactic diagrams. It determines which specialized data are required: decisions need branches proved exhaustive by S-Dich, payload constructors need typed payload data with S-Rout, certificates need C1-C5 certificate data, handoffs need consumer data checked by S-Trig, scope audits and admission gates need findings, and loop measures need S-Meas measure data.

nodeSpec.description: The static typed specification of one proof obligation or semantic runtime state. Diagram states, alternatives, and terminal exits are represented with explicit inputs, outputs, contracts, dependencies, and exits.

artifactType.description: The tactic artifact discriminator; it must be exactly 'tacticSpec'.

schemaVersion.description: The version of the tactic-specification format; it must match all contained node artifacts and the containing library.

graphVersion.description: Optional semantic graph version, present when a tactic uses stable runtime-state IDs rather than only source-ordinal diagram IDs.

tacticId.description: A stable tactic identifier beginning with a letter and containing only letters, digits, underscores, or hyphens.

name.description: The nonempty mathematical name of the tactic or proof-flow module.

tacticType.description: The tactic's principal closure mechanism, selected from the framework's finite tactic taxonomy: local_test (CT1), minimality (CT2), external_type (CT3), charging (CT4), bookkeeping (CT5), dichotomy (CT6), exchange (CT7), pumping (CT8), overload (CT9), refinement (CT10), localization (CT11), loop (CT12), tiered_charging (CT13), aggregate (CT14), rank (CT15), exact_type (CT16), target_thickening (CT17), or generic for a new tactic built from the framework's template.

latex.description: A LaTeX rendering of the tactic signature, including its input, terminal certificate classes, intended payloads, and recovery payloads.

inputDescription.description: A complete prose description of the well-formed branch-state data required at entry.

parameterDescription.description: A complete prose description of supplied constructors, thresholds, orders, finite tables, theorem handles, or other tactic parameters and their admissibility requirements.

signature.description: The tactic's typed input/parameter/output interface, matching the framework's tactic-signature table: trigger payload or branch-state input, supplied parameters, terminal certificate classes, and intended and recovery payload routes.

execution.description: The ordered mathematical work performed after admission, including the contracts that justify every construction, split, comparison, or closure.

routing.description: Every legal terminal certificate and residual handoff, with enough information to match payload producers to their unique consumers.

limit.description: The exact inapplicability, execution-error, design-failure, and candidate-scope-obstruction conditions; per the framework, an unfilled or invalid schema is an execution error written to the defect register, and an unresolved proof must not be presented as a legal residual.

mechanism.description: The tactic's proof algorithm, legal routing, and boundary of applicability.

entryNodeIds.description: The nonempty unique set of nodes at which an admitted run may begin; each identifier must resolve to a node in this tactic.

nodes.description: The nonempty array of all node specifications in stable nodeNumber order; it must cover every mathematical state, decision, construction, check, recovery, and sink in the tactic.

transitions.description: The complete transition graph of the tactic; every endpoint and outgoingTransitionIds entry must resolve and every branch must be represented.

allowedCertificates.description: The unique C1-C5 terminal classes this tactic is authorized to return; every certificate node must use a listed class.

declaredPayloads.description: The unique payload IDs this tactic may construct; every payload alternative must be declared here and match a typed payload signature P_{i->j} with a unique consumer in the framework's interlock graph.

scopeCandidateNodeIds.description: The unique node IDs referred to the global proof-language scope audit of the framework's repair protocol; all must be scope_audit nodes carrying candidate scope obstructions.

auditRejectNodeIds.description: The unique node IDs that reject an invalid invocation or execution under the framework's admission gates; all must be audit_gate nodes.

numberingPolicy.description: The exact stable numbering convention for nodes and transitions, including the distinction between source order and execution order.

noUnnamedResiduals.description: Must be true: every non-closing branch must construct a named typed payload or enter an explicit audit path.

allSinksTyped.description: Must be true: every graph sink must be a certificate, consumer handoff, scope audit, or execution rejection.

uniqueConsumerPerPayload.description: Must be true: each declared payload has exactly one consumer in the interlock graph.

allDecisionBranchesExplicit.description: Must be true: each decision's exhaustive alternatives and corresponding outgoing transitions are explicitly enumerated.

completeness.description: Machine-checkable declarations that the static tactic graph has no unnamed or untyped branch residue. This is structural branch coverage, not a claim that mathematical witnesses can be found without supplied proof-producing plans.

relativeToSuppliedPlans.description: Must be true: runtime totality is relative to explicitly supplied proof-producing decision plans and makes no search-completeness claim.

terminalCases.description: The exact distinct terminal cases exhausted by the tactic's typed execution result.

statement.description: A nonempty explanation of the totality theorem and the supplied-plan assumptions under which it holds.

totality.description: Optional runtime-totality claim for a semantic machine, explicitly separated from structural all-node verification and mathematical search completeness.

hasCycles.description: Whether the transition graph contains any declared execution or repair-reentry cycle.

cycles.description: Each declared cycle as an ordered sequence of at least two node IDs; no undeclared graph cycle is permitted.

cycleKinds.description: For each declared cycle, its semantic class: an execution loop or repair re-entry.

requiresWellFoundedMeasure.description: Whether cycle execution requires S-Meas strict descent; this must be true whenever a nontrivial execution cycle can repeat, and per the framework the loop is terminating only when S-Meas and S-Rest are both discharged.

cyclePolicy.description: The declaration of all permitted graph cycles and the proof obligations required to make execution terminating.

file.description: The repository-relative source-document path containing the tactic specification or proof-flow module.

section.description: The source section or figure containing the complete tactic definition.

line.description: The one-based source line at which the tactic specification begins.

source.description: The location in the structural exhaustion framework document prescribing the tactic and its graph.

tacticSpec.description: A complete ordered obligation graph for one reusable closure tactic of the framework's tactic library (CT1-CT17 or a new tactic built from the framework template). Its node array supplies stable source order, while its transition array is the authoritative branching and execution relation.

artifactType.description: The library artifact discriminator; it must be exactly 'tacticLibrary'.

schemaVersion.description: The schema version shared by the library and all contained tactic specifications.

libraryId.description: A stable identifier for this tactic collection.

title.description: A human-readable title identifying the structural-exhaustion framework or proof instance represented by the collection.

sourceDocument.description: The repository-relative source document whose proof architecture and mathematical obligations the library encodes; for this package it is the structural exhaustion framework document defining the CT1-CT17 tactic library and strategy manual.

numberingConvention.description: The normative rule for stable tactic, node, and transition identifiers and for interpreting array order versus execution order.

tactics.description: The nonempty collection of complete tactic specifications; tactic IDs must be unique and all cross-tactic payload routes must resolve.

tacticLibrary.description: A versioned collection of closure-tactic specifications used to realize the structural-exhaustion proof architecture.

kind.description: The evidence category, distinguishing mathematical lemmas and definitions from calculations, executable code, tables, proof-assistant artifacts, reviews, black-box results, and audit records.

reference.description: A nonempty resolvable label, path, theorem name, table ID, code artifact, or audit-row ID identifying the evidence.

hash.description: When the evidence is an external or executable artifact, an optional content digest pinning the exact reviewed version.

notes.description: Optional qualification of what the cited evidence proves, which hypotheses it uses, or which portion of a larger artifact is relevant.

evidenceRef.description: A traceable reference to evidence that discharges a mathematical contract, such as a labelled lemma, definition, calculation, finite enumeration, code artifact, proof-assistant theorem, review record, black-box theorem, or audit row. It types one entry of the framework's audit record A, through which every proof step must be available to later branches.

schema.description: The contract discriminator; it must be exactly 'S-Prove'.

proposition.description: The exact proposition proved, including every hypothesis and quantified object used by the node.

assumptions.description: The explicit assumptions under which the proposition is proved; every item must already be present in the branch state or imported through cited evidence.

method.description: Optional concise identification of the proof method, reduction, cited theorem, or calculation used; it does not replace evidence.

evidence.description: The nonempty evidence set establishing the proposition from the stated assumptions.

contractSProve.description: S-Prove instance: evidence for an exact proposition used by a proof-flow node. S-Prove is a package extension of the framework's lemma-schema alphabet for displayed assertions; typical framework propositions are minimality consequences, forbidden-response statements, density bounds, rank bounds, monotonicity facts, capacity estimates, and exact-type equalities entered in the invariant register I.

schema.description: The contract discriminator; it must be exactly 'S-Def'.

subject.description: The typed object being defined and the role it acquires in the branch state.

sourceStateFields.description: The unique branch-state components from which the definition is constructed: baseline hypotheses H0, progress order, exclusions E, invariants I, residual condition R, vocabulary V, payload queue Q, or audit record A.

constructor.description: A nonempty exact constructor, formula, algorithm, selection rule, or source definition that determines the subject from the named state fields.

parameters.description: Every external parameter used by the constructor, such as a threshold, constant, theorem handle, root order, or finite bound.

tieBreaks.description: Every deterministic tie-break used by the constructor; per the framework's S-Det discipline, record the exact rule (for example a lexicographic choice) whenever a choice is possible.

interfaces.description: Every boundary, gluing interface, degree profile, or other compatibility interface on which the defined object depends.

contextClasses.description: Every outside-context class over which the definition or its target response is intended to be valid.

finiteAlphabets.description: Every finite label, exact-type, survivor, or certificate alphabet used by the construction, as required by the framework's label and external-type components (consumed by CT3, CT8, CT10, CT16).

evidence.description: The nonempty evidence set proving that the constructor is well-defined, admitted by the proof language, and has the declared sort.

contractSDef.description: S-Def instance: proof that a named object is constructed solely from recorded branch-state data using constructors admitted by the current proof language, with every parameter, tie-break, interface, context class, and finite alphabet exposed. This is the framework's S-Def definability lemma schema, naming its source data in H0, E, I, R, or V.

schema.description: The contract discriminator; it must be exactly 'S-Equiv'.

left.description: The first target-response predicate, representation, test, type, or support profile being compared.

right.description: The second predicate or representation claimed to have the same target-relevant response as left.

contextClass.description: The complete class of compatible outside contexts over which equivalence is asserted; it may not be replaced by a sample of contexts.

responseCriterion.description: The exact target-relevant observation that both sides must agree on, stated through the framework's target predicate T or its local test family and interface data.

directionsProved.description: When supplied, the two unique directions left_to_right and right_to_left; a full equivalence requires both.

evidence.description: The nonempty evidence set proving response equality on the entire declared context class.

contractSEquiv.description: S-Equiv instance: proof that two representations have identical target-relevant response on every declared compatible context. This is the framework's S-Equiv response-equivalence lemma schema: a type decides every compatible target-relevant context named by the interface, a local test family represents the target predicate T, and a finite context generator decides all-context equivalence for an exchange pair (used by CT1, CT3, CT7, CT8, CT16).

schema.description: The contract discriminator; it must be exactly 'S-Dich'.

residualClass.description: The exact current class of objects/states to which the split applies, after all recorded hypotheses, exclusions, invariants, and residual conditions are imposed.

condition.description: The mathematical condition defining this alternative, with enough precision to prove coverage and evaluate it from recorded data.

measurableFromState.description: Must be true, certifying that the alternative can be decided from the recorded branch state without hidden information.

kind.description: The destination class: another node, a terminal certificate, a typed payload, a scope audit, or an execution rejection.

reference.description: A nonempty ID resolving to the destination node, certificate class/record, payload, scope-audit node, or audit-reject node.

feeds.description: The typed destination produced when this alternative holds.

alternatives.description: At least two explicit branch-state-measurable alternatives whose union covers the residual class; each alternative must route to a declared continuation or closure.

pairwiseDisjoint.description: Optional declaration of whether the alternatives are pairwise disjoint; false is permitted only when overlap does not make routing ambiguous.

evidence.description: The nonempty evidence set proving exhaustive coverage and the validity of every advertised route.

contractSDich.description: S-Dich instance: proof that the listed, branch-state-measurable alternatives exhaust the current residual class and that each surviving alternative names its next node, certificate, payload, scope audit, or audit rejection. This is the framework's S-Dich exhaustive-split lemma schema over the residual class C(B), used by every decision node.

schema.description: The contract discriminator; it must be exactly 'S-Pers'.

fromStateRef.description: The nonempty identifier of the certified incoming branch state.

toStateRef.description: The nonempty identifier of the outgoing branch state after the transformation.

hypotheses.description: Baseline and branch hypotheses from H0 preserved by the transition, including the theorem hypotheses and target avoidance where applicable.

exclusions.description: Previously certified absent exits from the exclusion register E that remain valid after the transition.

invariants.description: Invariant-register entries that remain valid, such as minimality consequences, forbidden-response statements, density bounds, rank bounds, monotonicity facts, capacity estimates, or exact-type equalities.

residualConditions.description: Typed residual conditions R still defining the surviving subcase after the transition.

vocabularyEntries.description: Defined symbols, theorem handles, payload components, and other typed records of the vocabulary record V that remain in scope.

preserved.description: The complete downstream-relevant portion of the incoming state proved to survive in the outgoing state.

strengthenedInvariants.description: New or stronger invariants certified by this transition and added to the outgoing state's invariant register I.

newlyAbsentExits.description: Branch alternatives proved impossible by this transition and added to the exclusion register E.

evidence.description: The nonempty evidence set proving every listed preservation and strengthening claim.

contractSPers.description: S-Pers instance: proof that every downstream hypothesis, exclusion, invariant, residual condition, and vocabulary entry survives a state transition B -> B', with all strengthenings and newly excluded exits recorded. This is the framework's S-Pers persistence lemma schema for branch-state transitions.

schema.description: The contract discriminator; it must be exactly 'S-Det'.

choiceFunction.description: The exact total map or selection rule claimed to be canonical from recorded state, including its domain and codomain.

recordedDataDependencies.description: The nonempty list of branch-state fields and previously defined objects on which the choice is allowed to depend.

totalityStatement.description: The proposition that the choice function is defined for every admissible input in the current residual class.

tieBreakRule.description: When choices are not intrinsically unique, the complete deterministic tie-break rule (for example a lexicographic rule over recorded data), as required by the framework whenever a canonical assignment such as a ledger's charge map pi is used.

canonicalityUse.description: The downstream multiplicity, comparison, minimal-support, or reproducibility claim that relies on this choice being canonical; per the framework, S-Det is required whenever a later argument charges multiplicity through the choice or compares objects selected by it.

evidence.description: The nonempty evidence set proving determinism, permitted dependence, and totality.

contractSDet.description: S-Det instance: proof that a choice, representative, assignment, payer, support, label, or tie-break is a total function of recorded branch-state data. This is the framework's S-Det deterministic-choice lemma schema, used for canonical charge maps, label assignments, and tier assignments (CT4, CT5, CT13).

schema.description: The contract discriminator; it must be exactly 'S-Rout'.

payloadId.description: The nonempty stable identifier of the residual payload constructed by the producer.

producerTacticId.description: The nonempty identifier of the tactic/node that constructs the payload from its current branch state.

consumerTacticId.description: The nonempty identifier of the single tactic authorized to consume the payload.

tupleEntries.description: The nonempty ordered typed payload tuple; its component names, sorts, and roles must match the declaration and consumer signature.

vocabularyVerified.description: Must be true, certifying that every tuple entry is a defined object in the producer's current vocabulary.

uniqueConsumerVerified.description: Must be true, certifying that the interlock graph assigns this payload to exactly the named consumer.

evidence.description: The nonempty evidence set for construction, tuple well-formedness, vocabulary membership, and unique routing.

contractSRout.description: S-Rout instance: proof that a producer tactic CTi constructs a declared typed residual tuple matching a payload signature P_{i->j} and sends it to exactly one compatible consumer CTj. This is the framework's S-Rout payload-construction lemma schema; a residual with no declared consumer is a compile failure, not a terminal branch.

schema.description: The contract discriminator; it must be exactly 'S-Trig'.

payloadId.description: The nonempty identifier of the incoming payload being checked.

consumerTacticId.description: The nonempty identifier of the consumer whose declared trigger signature is being checked.

expectedArity.description: The nonnegative number of components required by the consumer signature; it must equal the actual payload tuple length.

position.description: The component's one-based position in the ordered payload tuple.

expectedSort.description: The mathematical sort required at this tuple position by the consumer signature.

actualRef.description: The vocabulary reference supplied at this tuple position by the producer.

valid.description: Must be true, certifying that the referenced component has the expected sort.

componentChecks.description: One positional check per payload component, recording the expected sort, actual reference, and successful type judgment.

standingHypotheses.description: Every baseline or residual hypothesis the consumer requires and that the incoming state is proved to satisfy.

interfaceChecks.description: Compatibility checks for boundaries, degree profiles, gluing data, labels, contexts, or other interfaces required by the consumer.

certificateHandleChecks.description: Checks that every theorem, computation, audit, or prior certificate handle required by the consumer is present and valid.

evidence.description: The nonempty evidence set establishing all trigger checks before consumer execution.

contractSTrig.description: S-Trig instance: proof, performed before a consumer executes, that an incoming payload P_{i->j} has the expected arity and sorts and preserves every required hypothesis, interface datum, and certificate handle. This is the framework's S-Trig consumer-trigger lemma schema, discharged whenever a payload is consumed from the payload queue Q.

schema.description: The contract discriminator; it must be exactly 'S-Comp'.

comparison.description: The exact equality, inequality, order relation, finite exhaustion, capacity bound, rank bound, or arithmetic statement being proved.

comparisonKind.description: The comparison's mathematical category, used to select the required checking discipline.

conclusionKind.description: Whether the proved comparison directly validates a terminal certificate, constructs a residual payload, or establishes an intermediate invariant.

conclusionRef.description: A nonempty identifier of the certificate, payload, or intermediate result justified by the comparison.

assumptions.description: Every explicit hypothesis, asymptotic regime, constant definition, prior bound, or admissibility condition used in the comparison.

evidence.description: The nonempty evidence set, such as a labelled proof, exact calculation, verified finite table, or code artifact, establishing the comparison.

contractSComp.description: S-Comp instance: proof of a numerical, finite, order, capacity, rank, or arithmetic comparison. This is the framework's S-Comp comparison lemma schema; typical instances are ledger closure inequalities sum w(d) >= l > sum kappa(p) (CT4), budget-functional sign checks (CT5, CT11), pigeonhole overload bounds (CT9), finite-table exhaustion counts (CT10), rank-budget comparisons (CT15), aggregate mass bounds (CT14), and arithmetic offset estimates (CT17). Its conclusion must be a C1-C5 certificate inequality or feed a declared residual payload.

schema.description: The contract discriminator; it must be exactly 'S-Rest'.

fromStateRef.description: The nonempty identifier of the branch state before the loop/repair pass.

toStateRef.description: The nonempty identifier of the restored state supplied to the next pass.

restoredSignature.description: The exact tactic-entry signature that the post-pass state must satisfy.

baselineHypotheses.description: The theorem and counterexample hypotheses restored unchanged for the next pass.

admissibilityConditions.description: The structural, boundary, target-avoidance, and other conditions that make the next pass legal.

invariants.description: The structural and quantitative facts retained or re-established after the pass.

ledgers.description: The demand, capacity, budget, rank, defect, slack, or other accounting records available to the next pass.

payloadHandles.description: Queued or active typed payload identifiers and certificate handles preserved for subsequent consumption.

restoredItems.description: The complete state components required for the next pass and proved present after restoration.

evidence.description: The nonempty evidence set proving that the restored state satisfies the complete signature.

contractSRest.description: S-Rest instance: proof that a non-closing loop or repair pass restores the complete branch-state signature required by its next pass. This is the framework's S-Rest loop-restoration lemma schema; together with S-Meas it certifies termination of iterative tactics such as CT12 (Peeling loop).

schema.description: The contract discriminator; it must be exactly 'S-Meas'.

measure.description: The exact measure as a function of recorded branch-state data, including its domain.

sourceData.description: The nonempty list of recorded state fields and typed objects from which the measure is computed.

wellFoundedCodomain.description: The codomain and strict relation, together with the assertion that the relation is well founded.

strictDecrease.description: The proposition that every non-closing pass maps the current measure to a strictly smaller value.

nonClosingPassRef.description: Optional stable identifier of the transition, loop body, refinement, peeling step, or recursion whose strict decrease is proved.

evidence.description: The nonempty evidence set proving definability, well-foundedness, and strict descent.

contractSMeas.description: S-Meas instance: proof that an iterative step is controlled by a measure derived from recorded data, valued in a well-founded order, and strictly decreasing on every non-closing pass. This is the framework's S-Meas well-founded-descent lemma schema for loops, peels, refinements, and recursions; together with S-Rest it certifies termination.

schema.description: The contract discriminator; it must be exactly 'A-Cert'.

certificateClass.description: The terminal class being validated: C1 direct target, C2 minimality/compression, C3 routed defect, C4 bounded capacity, or C5 finite verified impossibility.

validationStatement.description: The exact closing proposition: a target witness, smaller admissible object, accepted defect account, impossible comparison, or verified empty finite survivor set.

witnessRefs.description: The nonempty references to the concrete target, replacement, defect payload, inequality data, finite table, or other witnesses establishing the certificate.

requiredContractRefs.description: The nonempty references to all S-* contract instances on which certificate validity depends.

auditRowRefs.description: Audit entries of the framework's audit record A recording dependency, calculation, table, code-hash, slack, defect, or resilience information for this closure.

evidence.description: The nonempty final evidence set validating both the closing statement and its dependency chain.

contractACert.description: A-Cert instance: validation of a terminal C1-C5 certificate and of every witness and contract on which it depends. A-Cert is a package extension operationalizing the framework's certificate alphabet and leaf-totality rule: C1 direct target certificate, C2 minimality or compression certificate, C3 routed defect certificate, C4 bounded-capacity certificate, and C5 finite verified impossibility certificate.

schema.description: The contract discriminator; it must be exactly 'A-Scope'.

failedContract.description: The specific S-* or A-* contract whose required mathematical object or proof cannot be expressed; it must identify the real interface failure rather than a generic difficulty.

proofLanguage.description: A nonempty precise description or versioned identifier of the current proof language L = (Sorts, Constructors, Predicates, Measures, Certificates, Payloads, BlackBoxes) whose expressive limit is claimed.

nonExpressibilityStatement.description: The proposition that the required contract cannot be formulated or discharged in the current language for the stated residual class.

attemptedRepairs.description: The nonempty set of concrete definition, routing, refinement, type-extension, or other repairs attempted before declaring a scope candidate.

languageExtensionConsidered.description: Whether an explicit level-4 extension of the proof language was considered as a repair under the framework's repair protocol, with the decision documented in evidence.

evidence.description: The nonempty audit and mathematical evidence supporting non-expressibility and the failure of attempted repairs.

contractAScope.description: A-Scope instance: proof that a failed contract is genuinely inexpressible in the current proof language after concrete repairs have been attempted; a difficult or unfinished proof is not sufficient. A-Scope is a package extension operationalizing the framework's candidate-scope-obstruction audit and repair protocol, under which a candidate becomes a scope obstruction only after a level-4 language extension is ruled out.

schema.description: The contract discriminator; it must be exactly 'A-Admission'.

invocationRef.description: The nonempty identifier of the attempted tactic invocation or run rejected before execution.

failedConditions.description: The nonempty exact admission or trigger conditions that are false, missing, ill-typed, stale, or uncertified.

defectRegisterRef.description: The nonempty defect-register entry that owns and records this execution error.

remediation.description: Optional concrete action required to make a future invocation admissible; it must not disguise the failed execution as a mathematical residual.

evidence.description: The nonempty evidence set showing that the stated admission conditions failed.

contractAAdmission.description: A-Admission instance: an execution-rejection record created when a tactic invocation fails a stated precondition before the tactic begins. A-Admission is a package extension operationalizing the framework's admission gates: the both-sides test (payer and account, bounded residual, measurability in the branch state, typed consumer route) and the bookkeeping-vs-theorem test; the rejection is written to the defect register.

contractInstance.description: Exactly one fully populated S-* or A-* contract instance, selected by its schema discriminator. The S-* variants type the framework's lemma-schema alphabet; the A-* variants and S-Prove are package extensions for certificate validation, scope audit, admission, and displayed propositions.

artifactType.description: The proof-record discriminator; it must be exactly 'nodeProofRecord'.

schemaVersion.description: The proof-record schema version; it must be compatible with the referenced node and tactic specifications.

tacticId.description: The identifier of the tactic containing the discharged node.

nodeId.description: The exact node identifier whose obligation this record addresses.

nodeNumber.description: The node's one-based presentation-order number. It agrees with a legacy Nnn suffix when present; semantic runtime IDs are independent of this ordinal.

status.description: The node's current or final discharge state. Successful values must agree with the obligation modality; failed, rejected, and scope_candidate records require the corresponding failure/audit evidence.

dischargedContracts.description: The unique contract names actually discharged by this record; semantic validation requires equality with both the node's contractSchemas and the schema discriminators in contractInstances.

contractInstances.description: The complete structured S-* and A-* instances discharging the node's declared contracts.

evidence.description: Additional node-level evidence references not already localized inside a contract instance; every cited artifact must be resolvable.

resultSummary.description: A precise summary of the established definition, theorem, comparison, payload, certificate, or audit finding made available by the record.

definedSymbols.description: Stable names of all new vocabulary-record entries defined by this node; use only for definitions actually established by its contracts.

producedPayloads.description: Stable payload IDs actually constructed by this node; each must be declared by the tactic and supported by S-Rout.

certificate.description: The C1-C5 terminal certificate actually validated by this node, when it closes a branch.

class.description: The failure class from the framework's failure taxonomy: E for an execution/admission error written to the defect register, D for a repairable design defect, or S for a candidate proof-language scope obstruction referred to the global audit.

defectId.description: The stable defect-register identifier owning this failure and its remediation/audit history.

description.description: The exact failed condition and its mathematical effect, stated concretely enough to distinguish a missing proof, ill-typed datum, design gap, and genuine scope candidate.

failure.description: Structured failure information for failed, rejected, or scope-candidate records; it classifies the issue without treating it as a legal proof residual.

nodeProofRecord.description: Evidence and outcome for one node obligation or semantic runtime state. A successful record instantiates exactly the declared contracts and cites enough evidence to justify its result.

artifactType.description: The run artifact discriminator; it must be exactly 'tacticRun'.

schemaVersion.description: The run-record schema version; it must be compatible with the executed tactic and node proof records.

runId.description: A stable identifier for this particular tactic execution on one incoming branch state.

tacticId.description: The identifier of the tactic whose transition graph governs this run.

inputBranchStateRef.description: The identifier of the well-formed incoming branch-state snapshot admitted to the run.

visitedNodeIds.description: The nonempty execution-order sequence of visited node IDs; consecutive IDs must follow declared transitions and repetitions require declared cycles.

nodeRecords.description: The nonempty execution-order sequence of proof records corresponding one-for-one to visitedNodeIds.

kind.description: The outcome category, which must agree with the terminal role and result of the final visited node; under the framework's leaf-totality rule a run may end only in a C1-C5 certificate, a typed payload with a declared consumer, an admission rejection, or an audited scope candidate.

reference.description: The stable certificate, payload, admission-audit, or scope-audit identifier produced by the final node.

terminalCase.description: Optional exact semantic terminal-case discriminator. Runtime-state graphs use it to distinguish exits that share an outcome kind or consumer.

outcome.description: The single legal result of the run: terminal certificate, typed payload, execution rejection, or audited scope candidate.

outputBranchStateRef.description: For a nonterminal payload outcome, the identifier of the certified branch-state extension delivered to the consumer.

auditRecordRefs.description: Audit-row identifiers recording this execution's dependencies, decisions, comparisons, code artifacts, defects, or terminal validation.

x-semanticValidationRules: visitedNodeIds and nodeRecords have equal length
each record nodeId equals the corresponding visitedNodeIds item
the first node is an entry node
each consecutive pair follows a declared transition
the final node and outcome agree
loop repetitions are allowed only on declared cycles
tacticRun.description: One actual path through a tactic graph, from an admitted branch state to one certificate, typed payload, audited rejection, or scope candidate. It realizes the framework's runtime model (propose, admit, select, execute, route, record): it records only visited nodes and may repeat nodes only on declared cycles controlled by S-Meas and S-Rest.

artifactType.description: The verification artifact discriminator; it must be exactly 'tacticVerification'.

schemaVersion.description: The verification schema version; it must be compatible with the tactic and all included node records.

verificationId.description: A nonempty stable identifier for this complete all-node verification package.

tacticId.description: The nonempty identifier of the fully verified tactic.

nodeRecords.description: The nonempty complete array containing exactly one successful discharge record for every tactic node in nodeNumber order, including every branch and recovery obligation.

summary.description: A precise verification conclusion stating that all node contracts, graph completeness rules, payload routes, sinks, and cycle obligations have been checked.

reviewerRefs.description: Identifiers of human or automated reviewers responsible for mathematical, schema, or proof-artifact review.

auditRecordRefs.description: Audit-row identifiers supporting the tactic-level completeness and verification conclusion.

x-semanticValidationRules: contains exactly one successful discharge record for every tactic node
tacticVerification.description: A structural all-branches verification containing one successful discharge record for every node in presentation order. It proves branch and sink coverage for the supplied graph; it does not prove witness-search completeness.

x-semanticValidationRules: nodeNumber equals one-based array position and is consecutive presentation order
legacy node IDs equal <tacticId>.N<zero-padded nodeNumber>; semantic runtime IDs are checked against the tactic graph version
transitionNumber is consecutive presentation order and all endpoints exist; IDs may be ordinal or semantic
every node is reachable from an entry node
every sink is a certificate, consumer handoff, scope audit, or admission reject
every payload alternative has one declared consumer
every decision has all outgoing guards explicitly represented
every execution cycle has S-Meas and S-Rest obligations
all certificate classes and payload IDs are declared at tactic level
dischargedContracts equals the set of schema discriminators in contractInstances
